Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search for Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records with Javier Morales as the coordinator to identify Javier's active relocations, and separate them form completed work.
Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with the active relocation names to identify which tickets are complete, which remain open, and whether any open items are overdue or not accurately reflected on the current tracking.
Search emails using search_emails with queries for the active relocation names, clients, and related move details to find client-facing deadlines, pending decisions, vendor dependencies, and unresolved logistics.
Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to the active relocations, blockers, delays, workload, and related terms to find internal blockers, timing risks, and capacity concerns.
Cross-check Airtable, Linear, email, and Slack to determine the correct current state of Javier's active relocations and identify any mismatch between internal records and latest communications.
Look up the relevant client contacts using contacts_search_contacts for relocations that require time-sensitive follow-up.
Update the relevant internal tracking records using airtable_update_records so they reflect current status, remaining open work, and any risks or blockers discovered.
Add comments to the relevant Linear issues using linear_create_comment so the ticket history reflects what was found and what still needs action.
Send follow-up emails using send_email for any active relocation where the client is still waiting on a time-sensitive update or next step.
Post an urgent message in #operation using conversations_add_message only if the review uncovers an issue that needs immediate attention or escalation, including what was found and what action was already taken.

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
Search for Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records with Javier Morales as the coordinator to identify Javier's active relocations, and separate them form completed work.
Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with the active relocation names to identify which tickets are complete, which remain open, and whether any open items are overdue or not accurately reflected on the current tracking.
Search emails using search_emails with queries for the active relocation names, clients, and related move details to find client-facing deadlines, pending decisions, vendor dependencies, and unresolved logistics.
Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to the active relocations, blockers, delays, workload, and related terms to find internal blockers, timing risks, and capacity concerns.
Cross-check Airtable, Linear, email, and Slack to determine the correct current state of Javier's active relocations and identify any mismatch between internal records and latest communications.
Look up the relevant client contacts using contacts_search_contacts for relocations that require time-sensitive follow-up.
Update the relevant internal tracking records using airtable_update_records so they reflect current status, remaining open work, and any risks or blockers discovered.
Add comments to the relevant Linear issues using linear_create_comment so the ticket history reflects what was found and what still needs action.
Send follow-up emails using send_email for any active relocation where the client is still waiting on a time-sensitive update or next step.
Post an urgent message in #operation using conversations_add_message only if the review uncovers an issue that needs immediate attention or escalation, including what was found and what action was already taken.

13 The final response correctly identifies at least one of Javier's active relocations as at risk (e.g., Danielle Osei at risk due to three pending deadlines this week,)
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response identifies 'Danielle Osei (Vectral, Phoenix→Denver)' as HIGH SENSITIVITY with three pending items (apartment search, flight, movers) and attributes the risk to Vectral's active churn negotiations and the ExpenseBot incident — a specific named relocation with a specific at-risk reason.
✓Run #2The final response identifies multiple at-risk relocations with specific reasons: Keiko Tanaka (DOT hazmat / missing placarding certificate), Joaquín Reyes (building shutdown creating a tight 2-day delivery window), and Diego Castañeda (16-day storage gap not yet sourced).
✓Run #3The final response identifies multiple named relocations as at risk with specific reasons attached: NorthWind chain-of-custody template (deadline passed, client may switch vendors), GreenStack Mei-Lin Zhao hazmat storage (10-day notice window razor-thin), GreenStack Joaquín Reyes rig (narrow delivery window/capacity risk), and BrightLoop vehicle shipping (April 20 deadline unconfirmed).
✓Run #4The final response identifies Danielle Osei as CRITICAL/at-risk: Airtable incorrectly marked Completed while nothing was actually done, move date April 26-27, Vectral contract renewal at risk due to Rachel Whitfield's churn threat.
